findings.description: u_0002 states that all images must be housed in the Accent shape (asymmetric rounded-corner crops, not rectangles or circles) — a distinct, testable imagery rule referencing §2.5. No candidate rule captures this requirement; it is missing entirely from the cluster.

payload.text: Images must be housed in the Accent shape (asymmetric rounded-corner crops, per §2.5) — not plain rectangles or circles.

findings.description: The alt-text rule is flagged as a 'brand accessibility rule' (implying an external/governing policy) but the extracted rule carries no governance facet (e.g., policy reference, owner, or enforcement mechanism) despite being classed as a hard_constraint compliance item.

Source describes photography treatment as a definitive stylistic identity statement ('warm, realistic patient lifestyle imagery... real moments') with no modal softening language, yet it is tagged strong_default/should rather than reflecting its role as a core, consistently-applied brand imagery standard; hardness/polarity may be understated relative to source emphasis.
